tasks.name: N3: [RX validation] Debug the PMDR floor for data rate < 5Mbps without idle slots.
tasks.description:
Debug Results PPT: [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan\_10xar\_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX\_Testing/RX\_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:p:/r/personal/shovan_10xar_com/Documents/Tapeout/QS222/Measurement%20Results/RX_Testing/RX_Testing.pptx?d=we5fcb0e106fa4660b5f303f02e25350e&csf=1&web=1&e=b5gIsb&nav=eyJzSWQiOjcyNSwiY0lkIjoxMDQ5NTkwNzY2fQ)

This issue looks like a f/w issue.
With packet interval = Active slot, we see two problems
1. We only see 650 number of IRQs out of 1000. By increasing the packet interval (i.e. adding idle slot), we do not see this problem and the IRQs are as expected.
2. The SPI readout is not happening randomly even though the IRQ was provided by the chip.

These issues are to be checked in the f/w and Kumaresh will take this up.

Please find the scope captures :

IRQ received only 650 times out of 1000:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/fe2fd62a-9476-4a0d-95ea-07ad9dc880dd/image.png)

SPI readout not happening even though IRQ is present:
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/9fb8f69c-280d-465c-a0ed-b0e7d3aab0fb/image.png)

Resolution:
The issue was with the software where the wait time for 'test done' was being calculated in terms of the duration set in the GUI as below. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = duration \* pkt\_count + 5

So when we set the duration as 0 in the GUI for avoiding the idle slot, the wait time is 5sec (= 0 \* 1000 + 5). This time is not sufficient for lower data rates and hence the test was stopped before receiving all packets. 

We tested with the corrected code which takes in the IC interval (active slot + idle slot) instead of GUI duration setting. This resolved the issue. 

wait\_for\_test\_done = **IC interval** \* pkt\_count + 5
tasks.creator: Sarat Anumula

tasks.name: N3:[Baseband] Baseband testing - QAM, DSSS, Adiabatic in schedule mode with multi packets with & without Acks.
tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)

**Why:** Need to have QS222 validated in the baseband testing in Schedule mode with Multiple Packets in a single slot for a thorough validation of QS222.

**Deliverable:**
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
500 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1
1. QAM 2- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 3 in step of 1
2. QAM 4- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 4 in step of 1
3. QAM 16- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 5 in step of 1
4. QAM 64- packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800 dr\_scale = 0 to 5 in step of 1
5. DSSS QAM2 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 3,4
6. DSSS QAM4 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 3,4
7. Adiabatic QAM2 (BPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 3 in step of 1
8. Adiabatic QAM4 (QPSK) - packet size - 16, 64, 128, 256, 512, 1010, 1351,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 4 in step of 1

**Limitations:** We need to create the multipacket transmission script on both FW and SW before validating transmission in a single slot.

**Results:** Anish will provide an update here (link to the results) after validation is completed.

![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/890f49a4-98d0-4160-a71e-8fea6c841612/image.png)
\]
If the TX  is transmitting, then it can happen. 
I am assuming this is the en\_encoder = 0 mode. Then the TX starts transmitting while the SPI is still writing the packet.
In that case the register that the MISO line reads from (Basically same as the SRAM address the PHY is reading from), keeps changing and so the MISO line keeps toggling

tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)
deliverable:
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1

1. ADIABATIC QAM 2- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in steps of 1
2. ADIABATIC QAM 4- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 0 to 12 in steps of 1

This will be the first debug cycle. Run the test and report the bug. no need to debug. will create a separate task for debug.
**Risk:** NA
**Limitation:** Assumption: FW and config settings are correct
**Constraint:** If the active slot duration is greater than 2sec then, Transmitted packets are not being received. Suspicion is that FW is unable to handle that high slot duration. (might need more deep dive studies later for a better understanding).

Based on the limited testing done - here are the conditions (corner cases) where active slot duration > 2 sec:
a) QAM4 pkt size > 500 bytes. DR\_Scale = 12;
b) QAM2 pkt size > 250 bytes. DR\_Scale = 12

Dependency: Sayantan help is needed for debug of the Frame duration when IDLE slots are removed.
tasks.creator: Sarat Anumula

tasks.description:
doc:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-c9ca6436-5131-4f03-8e8f-38bf36240230)
deliverable:
Conditions: SPI -24 MHz, SYSCLK= 10 MHz ; Onboard supply.
100 packets - header on. a) no encoding, b) crc, c) rs+crc, max payload size 255, 127, and max parity 32, Header RS Parity = 1
1.DSSS QAM 2- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 3 to 10 insteps to 1
2.DSSS QAM 4- packet size - 16,64,255,512,1024,1800, dr\_scale = 3 to 10 insteps to 1

This will be the first debug cycle. Run the test and report the bug. no need to debug. will create a separate task for debug.
Risk: NA
Limitation: Assumption: FW and config settings are correct
Dependency: Sayantan help is needed for debug of the Frame duration when IDLE slots are removed.

**Results Summary:**
All listed conditions were tested under the following setup:
*   **Config:** SPI = 24 MHz, SYSCLK = 10 MHz, onboard supply
*   **Packets:** 100 packets with header enabled
    *   Modes: (a) no encoding, (b) CRC, (c) RS+CRC
    *   Payload sizes: 255, 127; max parity = 32; Header RS Parity = 1
*   **DSSS QAM‑2:** Packet sizes 16, 64, 255, 512, 1024, 1800; `dr_scale` = 3→10 (step 1)
*   **DSSS QAM‑4:** Packet sizes 16, 64, 255, 512, 1024, 1800; `dr_scale` = 3→10 (step 1)
**Issue:**​​
For **QAM‑4, Packet Size = 16**, the configuration with **Full CRC + RS** fails whenusing a payload size of **255** (en\_crc = True, en\_rs\_crc = True,max\_payload = 255).​

We faced few other issues like partial packet transmission. that is captured in the documents attached.
tasks.creator: Sarat Anumula

tasks.name: N3: [Other_Misc] Removal of buck_low_pwr_cfg_bits as reserved and making as accessible from GUI (enhancement to Python and excel change)
tasks.description:
**Why:** Config bits needs to have a mean to access by internal engineering teams for thorough validation of the QS222.

**Deliverable:** Need to make enhancements to presets to make the buck\_low\_pwr\_cfg\_bits to be able to access them. Currently this register is defined as reserved and hence the tool cannot access this register. Current workaround used by the team is to access them via SPI read/ write.

Required
0x0009: buck\_sel\_vref\_cntrl\_lp: bit\_width = 4: bit\_offset = 3
0x0013: buck\_sel\_rfb\_dig\_lp: bitt\_width = 4: bit\_offset = 0
0x0013: buck\_sel\_rfb\_ana\_lp: bitt\_width = 4: bit\_offset = 4

**Risks: (t)** - Limiting the accessibility to the config registers may not help internal product engineering team to thoroughly validate it.
tasks.creator: Sarat Anumula

tasks.description:
**Debug Note – Sync Slot Reception Issue**
During sync slot operation, RX was failing to receive packets.
Root cause identified: **rxphy\_rx\_cfg\_sync\_address** was not aligned with the TX-side destination address. Since sync slot reception depends on address matching, RX will only accept packets when **rxphy\_rx\_cfg\_sync\_address** equals the TX destination address. Updated the register configuration accordingly to ensure proper packet reception

tasks.description:
Deliverable:
docs:[](https://app.clickup.com/9017196379/v/dc/8cqezuv-18997/8cqezuv-39177?&block=block-8f7d35b9-541e-4c20-abb9-d0712481e839)
1. **CHIP\_EN**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
2. **VDDIO\_ANA**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
3. **VDDIO\_AON**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
4. **VDDIO\_BUCK**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
5. **VDDIO\_TX**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
6. **VDDIO\_TXANA**: 0 → 2.2V (IO voltage)
7. BYPASS\_PS = 2.2 (IO voltage)
8. EN\_EFUSERD\_N = 2.2V (IO voltage) (For non-efuse read bootup)
9. Check the supplies at:
    1. **BUCK\_OUT\_1P2** pin (1.2V)
    2. **BUCK\_0P8 = 1V**
    3. Signals at these ports indicate dc-dc and vdd\_aon working.
OBSERVATION: Check the BUCK\_OUT\_1P2 and BUCK\_0P8 pin.
expectation - BOUT\_OUT\_1P2 will be 1.2V and BUCK\_0P8 will be 1V.
Measurement: Vary the load current of 0.8V and 1.2V output and measure the efficiency of the dc-dc converter.

Capture the result in a slide and the slide here.

Risk: NA
Limitation: Assumption: input is properly given

**Following are the bench observations:** [https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ER-FaF8qVl1PmL2ByuSvNDcBDHs77eTIqGqKNbDvZSav3A?e=haK0HB](https://10xar-my.sharepoint.com/:x:/p/shovan/ER-FaF8qVl1PmL2ByuSvNDcBDHs77eTIqGqKNbDvZSav3A?e=haK0HB)
![](https://t9017196379.p.clickup-attachments.com/t9017196379/c08ec2ef-b719-441f-aef4-28f34d5c5b82/image.png)
Default voltages from SIMO
1p2 = 1.17V
0p8 = 0.98V

in order to get close to 1.2V, 0.8V respectively, following config is used:

```yaml
buck_sel_rfb_dig_nvm: 11
buck_sel_rfb_ana_nvm: 6
```

Post layout sims give **84% efficiency** for 1p2Buck = 1.2V, 0p8Buck = 0.8V,
For the same scenario on bench we are getting **~79% efficiency**
